title: T-128 — UI: Pipeline TableView (pipeline_table prototype) NPD-f slice of T-060
prompt:
# T-128 — Pipeline TableView slice

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §17.8 + UX prototypes/design/01-NPD-UX.md pipeline_table
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/pipeline.jsx:54-88 (TableView)
Prototype-index entry: pipeline (TableView region)
Parent root: T-060

## Goal
Single Client Component TableView translating the prototype TableView region into a production React Client Component. Renders a dense sortable table of NPD projects with 8 columns (Code, Name, Type, Current Gate, Priority, Owner, Target Launch, Progress), status badges, bulk-action toolbar, and URL persistence of sort state (?sort=&dir=). Consumes the project list from parent props; does not query the DB.

## Implementation contract
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/table-view.tsx ('use client'). Props: { projects: NpdProjectRow[], onSelect?: (id: string) => void }.
- Use shadcn Table + Badge + Button primitives; sortable column headers with chevron icons; bulk-actions toolbar rendered above the table when rows are selected (Checkbox primitive).
- URL persistence via Next.js useSearchParams + useRouter — sort + dir; do not mutate parent state directly.
- Status badge variants map to gate state (todo/in-progress/blocked/done) and priority badges (high/med/low) per prototype.
- No DB calls inside the component (parent RSC provides projects).

## Files (≤ 2)
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/table-view.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/__tests__/table-view.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given TableView renders with a sample project list, when compared to prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/pipeline.jsx:54-88 (TableView), then it has the same 8 columns (Code, Name, Type, Current Gate, Priority, Owner, Target Launch, Progress), status + priority Badges, and uses shadcn Table primitives (no HTML <table> sort attributes) — verified by RTL parity checklist
2. Given a user clicks a sortable header, when the click settles, then URL ?sort=<col>&dir=<asc|desc> updates and the rows re-render in the new order — verified by RTL router test
3. Given one or more rows are checked, when the toolbar renders, then bulk-action buttons (Assign Owner, Set Priority, Move Gate) appear via shadcn Button primitives — verified by RTL

## Test strategy
- RED: table-view.test.tsx asserting 8 columns, badge variants, sortable header URL update, bulk-actions toolbar visibility — before component exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test table-view'

## Risk red lines
- Do not use HTML <table> sort attributes — use shadcn Table primitives
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim
- Do not query DB from this Client Component (parent provides projects)
